old_claim.note: E-Commerce Platforms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Online-commerce marketplace practice supplies the transaction, auction, targeting, and settlement context for ad-impression markets; it is scaling and market infrastructure rather than a storefront prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: About Open RTB defines real-time bidding as transacting media by putting an individual ad impression up for bid in a real-time programmatic auction, similar to financial markets.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source frames RTB exchanges as programmatic transaction markets for digital media impressions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That market framing supports a commercial/scaling relationship to digital commerce practice, while the retype prevents storefront/catalog software from being treated as a direct technical prerequisite.

ontology_before: Digital Advertising Exchanges treated e-commerce platforms as a generic enabling prerequisite, which overstated the role of storefront-style commerce software.
ontology_after: Digital Advertising Exchanges use e_commerce_platforms only as commercial marketplace context while API-based RTB carries the concrete protocol mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: e_commerce_platforms is now a commercial_or_scaling_dependency for digital_advertising_exchanges, not a generic enabling edge.
would_reject_if: The digital_advertising_exchanges node were narrowed to retail-commerce storefront ad marketplaces only.
A source showed RTB exchange operation depended directly on online storefront, catalog, inventory, or logistics platform software.
A narrower digital_marketplace_auctions node were added and should replace e_commerce_platforms as the market-infrastructure prerequisite.
short_reason: RTB exchanges are digital marketplaces, not storefront systems.
